text:
An Olympic swimmer has been exiled from the games after flouting a major rule with her boyfriend while in Paris, according to the Brazilian Olympic Committee.

An Olympian has been banished from the games after sneaking away for a date with her boyfriend in Paris.

Brazilian swimmer Ana Carolina Vieira, 22, allegedly left the Village without permission to join her boyfriend Gabriel Santos, also an Olympic swimmer, to see the Eiffel Tower.

Her team’s leadership cottoned on to the breach from social media posts.

When confronted Santos reportedly apologised and was let off with a warning.

However, Vieira challenged a technical decision made by the Brazilian Olympic Committee (BOC) in what the sport's governing body described as a “disrespectful and aggressive manner”.

“The Brazilian Olympic Committee decided to punish the two athletes for having left the Olympic Village without authorization last Friday,” The BOC said in a statement.

"In addition to this fact, the athlete Ana Carolina, in a disrespectful and aggressive manner, contested the technical decision taken by the Brazilian Swimming Team committee.

Officials said Vieira “was punished with removal from the delegation” and would return to Brazil immediately.

Brazil's swim team leader Gustavo Otsuka told Reuters the swimmer’s attempt to raise her issue with the technical decision was "completely inappropriate".

"We can't play around here. She took a completely inappropriate position to make her point, her dismay, about the formation of the relay," he said.

"It was during this period that we decided to take this situation to the disciplinary committee, discussed it and took the appropriate action. We ended up finding out through the posts."

Vieira took to Instagram following the banishment, revealing she intends to seek legal action, according to reports.

She also claimed she had filed a suit of harassment within the team.

"I will speak to my lawyers, I promise to tell everything, I am sad, nervous, but with peace of mind because I know who I am. I know what my character and my nature are," she said.

"I hope to be able to defend Brazilian women's swimming with great pride. I ask for patience and thank you."

The Paris Olympic Games kicked off on Friday and will wrap up on August 11.

title: Brazilian swimmer Ana Vieira sent home from Paris Olympic Games after sneaking out of Village for date with boyfriend | Sky News Australia
description: An Olympian has been banished from the games after sneaking away for a date with her boyfriend in Paris.
